Package org.areslib.core.simulation

Class FrustumVisualizer

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.core.simulation.FrustumVisualizer
public class FrustumVisualizer extends Object
Frustum Visualizer for AdvantageScope 3D telemetry.

Intercepts the physical limits (FOV) of simulated cameras (e.g., Limelight) and structurally casts their bounding boxes into a WPILib Pose3d array. This array is natively logged using AdvantageKit and renders semi-transparent lines in the simulator showing mathematically exactly where the camera can see.

  • Method Details

    • generateFrustum

      public static Pose3d[] generateFrustum(Pose3d cameraPose, double horizontalFOV, double verticalFOV, double clipDistance)
      Generate an array of 5 3D poses representing a visual cone/frustum.
      Parameters:
      cameraPose - The exact physical mounting pose of the camera on the field (Robot Pose + Camera Transform).
      horizontalFOV - The horizontal FOV in degrees (e.g. Limelight 3 = 63.3)
      verticalFOV - The vertical FOV in degrees (e.g. Limelight 3 = 49.7)
      clipDistance - How far out to draw the bounding rays in meters.
      Returns:
      Pose3d array for AdvantageScope 3D rendering.

    • toFlatArray

      public static double[] toFlatArray(Pose3d[] poses)
      Helper method to flatten a Pose3d array into the AdvantageScope primitive double array format representing sequenced Quaternions: [x,y,z,w,i,j,k]
